name: Filing Criminal Complaint Checklist
description: Step-by-step checklist for filing criminal complaint and FIR with police

base_steps.action: Assess if criminal complaint is the right action
details: Consult with lawyer to understand if case qualifies as criminal
Discuss timeline and likely outcomes
Understand difference between FIR (criminal) and civil case
Clarify jurisdiction and which court/police station has authority

base_steps.action: Gather all evidence before filing complaint
details: Compile written timeline of all incidents with dates and times
Collect physical evidence (weapons, documents, recordings)
Get medical/injury reports if applicable
List all witnesses with contact information
Prepare written summary of complaint in simple language

base_steps.action: Visit police station and file FIR
details: Go to appropriate police station jurisdiction
Request to file First Information Report (FIR)
Narrate entire incident clearly and factually
Provide list of witnesses and their contact details
Firmly request FIR registration (log number to be given)
Obtain copy of FIR immediately for your records
Get officer's name, badge number, and station contact
Contact investigation officer 1-2 weeks after FIR filing
Check status of investigation
Provide any additional evidence discovered
Ask about timeline for investigation completion
Stay in touch and provide statements as required
You may be called for detailed statement/interrogation
Bring all relevant documents and evidence
Be accurate and factual in your statement
Consider taking lawyer's assistance if available
Request written copy of your statement
Respond promptly to police calls and summons
Attend any identification parades if required
Provide any additional information requested
Maintain contact with investigation officer
Track investigation progress

India.action: Apply for interim protection if you're in danger
details: Inform police about threats from accused
Request police protection/security
File application for protective custody if needed
Keep emergency contacts of police always
